title: Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Ewok Adventure

'''''Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Ewok Adventure''''' is a video game that was made, but never released, for the [[Wikipedia:Atari 2600|Atari 2600]] by [[Parker Brothers]]. It was intended to be one of two games based on [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] released in [[1983]], with ''[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Death Star Battle|Death Star Battle]]'' being the only released title.<ref name="SWI135" />

==Publisher's summary==
''You take the role of an EWOK in this thrilling game based on scenes from RETURN OF THE JEDI. Now a member of the Rebel cause, you must hang glide over dangerous territory in order to reach the Imperial shield generator. Your mission: to blow it apart!''<ref name="Atarimania" />

==Gameplay==
You use the analog stick to control a [[Hang-glider/Legends|hang glider]] flown by an [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]]. You use the button to drop rocks on the various enemies to destroy them. These include [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]], [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]], and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]]. If you hit them with a rock, they die in a fiery explosion. The objective of the game is to blow up the Imperial Base.

On the easier levels you can do this by flying your glider into the middle of the base, but on the more advanced levels, you have to hijack either an Imperial Speeder or AT-ST to destroy the base. In ''Ewok Adventure'', the Atari joystick is used similar to that of flight stick, allowing you to change the altitude and pitch of the glider. You have 360 degree control and can even fly side to side and backwards.

==Development==
Early materials from Parker Brothers list the game, referred to as '''''Star Wars: Revenge of the Jedi: Game I''''' or '''''Star Wars: Return of the Jedi: Game I''''', among the company's [[June]] [[1983]] releases, with a version for [[Wikipedia:Intellivision|Intellivision]] slated for that [[October]]. ''Death Star Battle'' was ''Game II''.<ref name="Atarimania" /> Later press showed the ''Ewok Adventure'' game's Atari 2600 version scheduled for [[November]] 1983.<ref name="TVGU-N83">{{BookCite|container=Availability Update|title=The Video Game Update|number=Volume 2, Number 8|date=November 1983|link=https://segaretro.org/Computer_Entertainer}}</ref>

Though prototype boxes were initially created with placeholder artwork for catalogs and advertisements, [[Tim Hildebrandt]] was commissioned to create the final cover art.<ref name="Gelberg" /><ref name="Galaxy5">{{GalaxyCite|5|Alone Together}}</ref>

A prototype of the finished game was found in [[1997]] and eventually released online.<ref name="Atariprotos">{{WebCite|url=http://www.atariprotos.com/2600/software/ewok/ewok.htm|text=Star Wars: Ewok Adventure|work=[http://www.atariprotos.com AtariProtos.com]|archivedate=20210401082649}}</ref>
